skill.name: opentargets-database
skill.description: Query the Open Targets Platform for target-disease associations, drug evidence, tractability, safety, and therapeutic target prioritization.
skill.summary: Retrieve Open Targets evidence for drug discovery and target assessment.
skill.author: K-Dense-AI

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution and weak cryptography alerts, but review found Markdown code fences, GraphQL examples, and biomedical terminology rather than dangerous execution paths. The confirmed residual risks are an executable Python helper and outbound requests to the public Open Targets GraphQL API. No evidence found of shell execution, credential access, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: Outbound Request to Public GraphQL API
low_findings.description: The helper posts GraphQL queries and variables to the Open Targets Platform endpoint. This is expected for the skill, but user-provided query content leaves the local environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network destination is hardcoded to the documented Open Targets API and the request is the core function. No credential collection or unrelated endpoint was found.

low_findings.title: Executable Helper Script with Example Main Block
low_findings.description: The Python file can run example Open Targets queries when executed directly. The examples print public biomedical results and do not invoke shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script has a main block and outbound network behavior, so it deserves disclosure. Review found no filesystem writes, environment access, or shell execution.

low_findings.title: Static Command Alerts Were False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command alerts are caused by Markdown code fences and inline field names in documentation. No subprocess, shell, eval, or os.system usage was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited regions are Python or GraphQL examples in Markdown documentation. A targeted search found no executable shell interfaces in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Crypto Alerts Were False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts did not correspond to cryptographic functions. The flagged areas describe diseases, scores, and GraphQL fields, not hashing or encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or cryptographic API usage was found during targeted review. The static matches appear to be substring or context-free matches.

content.user_title: Query Open Targets for Drug Discovery Evidence
content.value_statement: Drug discovery teams need fast access to target, disease, and drug evidence. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through focused Open Targets queries.
actual_capabilities: Search Open Targets entities by gene, disease, or drug name.
Retrieve target annotations including tractability, safety, and genetic constraint.
Collect target-disease evidence across genetics, drugs, pathways, expression, and literature.
Find known drugs for a disease and review target mechanisms.
Explain association scores and evidence quality for target prioritization.
Provide reference guidance for custom GraphQL queries.
limitations: It depends on the public Open Targets Platform API and its availability.
It is best for exploratory queries, not large systematic screens.
Association scores rank evidence strength and do not predict clinical success.
Biomedical conclusions still require expert review and primary source validation.

use_cases.title: Prioritize Targets for a Disease
use_cases.description: Identify high-scoring genes, review evidence types, and check tractability and safety signals.

use_cases.title: Assess a Candidate Gene
use_cases.description: Summarize disease associations, known drugs, genetic constraint, and target validation evidence.

use_cases.title: Map Drug Repurposing Signals
use_cases.description: Find known drugs for an indication and compare mechanisms, targets, and clinical phases.

prompt_templates.title: Find a Disease Identifier
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Open Targets for Alzheimer disease. Return the best disease identifier and explain why it is the right match.

prompt_templates.title: Profile a Target
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Open Targets to profile BRAF. Summarize tractability, safety liabilities, genetic constraint, and strongest disease associations.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Evidence for Targets
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the top Open Targets evidence for three candidate genes in asthma. Rank them and explain the main evidence drivers.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Custom Evidence Query
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Open Targets GraphQL query plan for a target-disease pair. Include evidence types, pagination, and interpretation checks.

output_examples.input: Find evidence for BRAF in melanoma.
output_examples.output: A concise evidence summary with target identity, disease match, leading data types, known drug context, and interpretation notes.

output_examples.input: Which drugs are known for Alzheimer disease?
output_examples.output: A ranked overview of known drugs, mechanisms, clinical phase context, and limitations for repurposing analysis.

output_examples.input: Assess whether a target has safety concerns.
output_examples.output: A target safety brief covering liabilities, tissue expression, genetic constraint, and flags requiring expert review.

best_practices: Start with exact Open Targets identifiers before comparing evidence.
Review evidence breakdowns, not only aggregate association scores.
Validate important findings against primary databases and publications.
anti_patterns: Do not treat association scores as clinical success probabilities.
Do not use repeated API calls for large-scale systematic screens.
Do not rely on literature text-mining alone for target validation.

faq.question: What data source does this skill use?
faq.answer: It uses the public Open Targets Platform GraphQL API and related reference guidance.

faq.question: Does it require an API key?
faq.answer: No. The referenced Open Targets GraphQL API does not require authentication.

faq.question: Can it rank targets for a disease?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare association scores, evidence types, tractability, and safety context.

faq.question: Can it run large batch analyses?
faq.answer: It is designed for exploratory queries. Use data downloads or BigQuery for large studies.

faq.question: Are results ready for clinical decisions?
faq.answer: No. Results support research prioritization and require expert validation before clinical use.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
